text: Large Language Models (LLMs) are deep learning models trained on massive text corpora using transformer architectures to understand and generate human language.

expected_summary.quick_summary: LLMs are transformer-based models trained on massive datasets to understand and generate language.

ai_pm_lens.business_value: Powers GenAI products.
ai_pm_lens.tradeoff: Quality vs cost and latency.

interview_qa.question: What is an LLM?
interview_qa.answer: A transformer-based model trained on large text corpora.

expected_summary.memory_hook: LLMs predict the next token at scale.

text: Tokenization converts raw text into smaller units that models can process.

expected_summary.quick_summary: Tokenization converts text into model-readable tokens.

ai_pm_lens.business_value: Enables model processing.
ai_pm_lens.tradeoff: More tokens increase cost.

interview_qa.question: Why are tokens important?
interview_qa.answer: Cost, latency and context limits are token-based.

expected_summary.memory_hook: Tokens are the currency of LLMs.

text: Embeddings represent text as vectors that capture semantic meaning.

expected_summary.quick_summary: Embeddings convert text into semantic vectors.

ai_pm_lens.business_value: Drives search and recommendations.
ai_pm_lens.tradeoff: Quality vs storage and retrieval cost.

interview_qa.question: What are embeddings used for?
interview_qa.answer: Semantic search, RAG and similarity matching.

expected_summary.memory_hook: Embeddings convert language into math.

text: Prompt engineering improves model outputs through better instructions.

expected_summary.quick_summary: Prompt engineering optimizes instructions to improve responses.

ai_pm_lens.business_value: Low-cost quality improvement.
ai_pm_lens.tradeoff: Prompt complexity vs maintainability.

interview_qa.question: Why use prompt engineering?
interview_qa.answer: Improve output quality without retraining.

expected_summary.memory_hook: Better prompts create better outputs.

text: Fine-tuning adapts a pretrained model to a specific domain.

expected_summary.quick_summary: Fine-tuning specializes a model for a target task.
expected_summary.flow: Pretrained Model → Domain Data → Fine-Tuned Model

ai_pm_lens.business_value: Improves domain accuracy.
ai_pm_lens.tradeoff: Better quality but higher training cost.

interview_qa.question: When should you fine-tune?
interview_qa.answer: When prompting alone cannot achieve required performance.

expected_summary.memory_hook: Fine-tuning teaches specialization.

text: Retrieval-Augmented Generation combines retrieval and generation.

expected_summary.quick_summary: RAG grounds model outputs using retrieved knowledge.

ai_pm_lens.business_value: Improves factual accuracy.
ai_pm_lens.tradeoff: Extra retrieval latency.

interview_qa.question: Why use RAG?
interview_qa.answer: To reduce hallucinations and use current data.

expected_summary.memory_hook: RAG equals Search plus Generation.

text: Hallucinations occur when models generate incorrect information.

expected_summary.quick_summary: Hallucinations are confident but incorrect model outputs.

ai_pm_lens.business_value: Major enterprise risk.
ai_pm_lens.tradeoff: Accuracy vs speed and cost.

interview_qa.question: How do you reduce hallucinations?
interview_qa.answer: Use RAG, validation and fine-tuning.

expected_summary.memory_hook: Hallucinations are AI's confident mistakes.

text: The context window defines how much information a model can consider.

expected_summary.quick_summary: Context window is the model's working memory.

ai_pm_lens.business_value: Supports longer tasks.
ai_pm_lens.tradeoff: Larger windows cost more.

interview_qa.question: Why does context matter?
interview_qa.answer: It determines how much information the model can retain.

expected_summary.memory_hook: Context window equals memory size.

text: AI agents combine LLMs with planning, memory and tools.

expected_summary.quick_summary: Agents use LLMs plus tools to perform actions.

ai_pm_lens.business_value: Automates workflows.
ai_pm_lens.tradeoff: Autonomy vs control.

interview_qa.question: Agent vs chatbot?
interview_qa.answer: Agents can take actions, chatbots mainly respond.

expected_summary.memory_hook: Chatbots answer; agents act.

text: LLM evaluation measures quality, safety and performance.

expected_summary.quick_summary: Evaluation determines whether AI systems perform well.

ai_pm_lens.business_value: Enables reliable AI deployment.
ai_pm_lens.tradeoff: Evaluation depth vs effort.

interview_qa.question: What metrics matter?
interview_qa.answer: Accuracy, latency, cost and user satisfaction.

expected_summary.memory_hook: If you can't measure it, you can't improve it.
